full_text:
Postdoctoral Fellowship in Spatial Biology of Tumor Ecosystems

Are you passionate about the tumor microenvironment and spatial biology in cancer? Join us in our mission to advance ovarian cancer research through groundbreaking spatial biology innovations.

Position Details:

- Fully salaried 3-year postdoctoral position in the Research Program in Systems Oncology, Faculty of Medicine, University of Helsinki.

- Application Deadline 30.11.2024

About Our Lab:

The Färkkilä Lab is at the forefront of high-impact cancer research, focusing on high-grade serous ovarian cancer (HGSC). We utilize cutting-edge spatial profiling technologies to investigate the complex interactions within the tumor microenvironment (TME). By integrating advanced imaging, robust bioinformatics, and tumor organoid models, our goal is to uncover novel immunomodulatory pathways and advance precision oncology.

Our lab fosters a collaborative and vibrant research culture, combining rigorous science with team-building activities. We emphasize efficient collaborations and flexible work hours to promote a healthy work-life balance. We do not conduct animal research, supporting our animal-friendly values. Discover more about our team spirit and ongoing projects on Twitter (https://x.com/systemtumor), and our website (https://farkkilab.org/).

Your Mission:

This postdoctoral position is tailored for an ambitious scientist eager to develop novel methods in spatial biology, aiming for a future group leader position.

- Method Development Focus: You will have the freedom to design and implement innovative spatial analysis techniques, contributing to advancements in cancer research.

- Scientific Independence: The project will be personalized to align with your expertise, exploring intersections between your skills and our research focus.

- Leadership Opportunities: Oversee and mentor a diverse team, gaining valuable project management and leadership experience.

- High-Impact Research: Leverage extensive resources, including clinical trial samples and a wealth of matched and paired datasets, to generate influential publications.

- Multidisciplinary Environment: Collaborate with experts in tumor biology, histopathology, image and transcriptomics analysis, as well as clinicians.

Key Responsibilities:

- Develop and lead projects centered on innovative spatial biology methods, driving new understanding of the TME.

- Collaborate in an interdisciplinary team, utilizing advanced imaging and bioinformatics tools to study tumor-immune interactions.

- Mentor students from various backgrounds and contribute to producing high-impact publications.

- Foster collaborations that advance the lab’s mission and ensure successful project outcomes.

Desired Qualifications:

- PhD in a relevant field (e.g. tumor biology, immunology, histopathology, or computational biology).

- Expertise in imaging technologies, single-cell analysis, proteomics, or spatial profiling.

- Strong proficiency in bioinformatics and data analysis, with experience handling complex biological datasets.

- Collaborative attitude, and the drive to pursue scientific independence.

What We Offer:

- Flexible Work Environment: We prioritize efficient collaborations over rigid working hours, and offer flexible working hours to support productivity and well-being.

- Career Advancement: Gain hands-on experience in leadership, mentorship, and project management in a dynamic setting.

- Scientific Freedom: Enjoy the opportunity to develop and refine new spatial biology methods with a high degree of autonomy.

- Collaborative Team Culture: Be part of a supportive, diverse team that values both impactful science and a balanced life.

- Salary will be determined based on the candidate’s skills and experience, in accordance with the university's salary system, and is expected to range from 3700 to 4000€ per month. This ensures a high quality of life in Helsinki.

How to Apply:

To apply, fill the application at the University of Helsinki website (www.------) and attach to your application:

- Cover letter including three referees (max 1 page)

- CV (max 2 pages)

- Publication list
